mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods show no significant outperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fail to outperform baseline in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5.5% or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1/HOUST growth or Rank IC < 0.04 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side disruptions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to inflationary pressures.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant underperformance in INDPRO contraction scenarios or Rank IC > -0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers inflation expectations, prompting Fed rate hike anticipation and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant underperformance when CPIAUCSL > 2.5% or Rank IC > -0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weakening labor demand, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant underperformance in PAYEMS contraction scenarios or Rank IC > -0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in consumer credit (RSAFS) above its 3-month average signals elevated household spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS growth or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) and stable FEDFUNDS rates indicates accommodative policy lag, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through delayed rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant outperformance in CPI/FEDFUNDS divergence scenarios or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.
